domain: Mongolian khoomei throat singing (single singer produces fundamental + selected high overtone partial simultaneously; vocal-tract isolates 6/7/8/9/10/12/13th partial; two pitches at the same time from one airflow)
specific_mechanism: Single airflow source decomposed into low-band fundamental + high-band selected overtone; frequency-disjoint so they do not mask; bandwidth allocated independently.
llm_application: KHOOMEI-DUAL: single transformer trunk produces TWO simultaneous output streams in DISJOINT vocabulary subspaces — low-band = standard text tokens; high-band = structured metadata tokens (e.g., citation, tag, confidence-score, retrieval-link) drawn from a non-overlapping ID range. Both streams decoded in ONE inference pass through paired output heads sharing the trunk; runtime choice of which secondary stream is active.
novelty_claim: Multi-task heads (T5, multi-objective LMs) co-train multiple outputs but typically with separate decoding passes or task tags. PDT synchronizes parallel streams via shared latent. Citation/tag generation in LLMs is typically inline as text or post-hoc. KHOOMEI-DUAL differs by SINGLE-PASS DUAL-STREAM DECODE in VOCABULARY-DISJOINT subspaces from a SHARED TRUNK with frequency-style band allocation — neither stream pollutes the other's vocabulary; structured second stream comes from non-overlapping token IDs.
